The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at its upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). I will increase the spreader width by 20% (within the 1.5x limit) to pull more heat away from the chip center towards the cooled boundaries.

runs.llm_decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C) by ~4.26°C. Previous attempts to increase spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500.0). The optimization history suggests shifting to geometry changes. Increasing the heat spreader height is the most effective remaining lever to improve vertical thermal conduction from the chip to the spreader without violating domain bounds or overlap constraints.
